entries.description: Registered Session 1 start via ai_router.start_session with orchestrator metadata (claude / anthropic / claude-opus-4-8, effort high). Verified the on-disk orchestrator block matches the actual orchestrator (no SessionStart-hook mis-attribution this time).

Surveyed every live shared-doc surface (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, docs/repository-reference.md, project-guidance.md, release-process.md, marketplace-release-process.md, review-criteria/set.md + spec.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md). Discovered the substantive migration was already applied out of band in commit e5a3476 ("misc fixes to guidance.") before the audit ran, with session-state.json still showing both sessions not-started. Ran a repo-wide grep for live references treating an engine file as the canonical source of shared/version/consumer facts.

Authored s1-audit-record.md (the authoritative S1 record): situational finding on the out-of-band commit, ratification of all four open design questions to their recommended option, the locked documentation-authority contract, verification that e5a3476 is complete and faithful, and the residual S2 scope (validation + consumer-table header nit). Added an S1 Audit Lock summary to spec.md.

Ran end-of-session cross-provider verification on the S1 audit bundle (s1-audit-record.md + the actual migrated CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md/repository-reference.md/project-guidance.md). Verifier: gemini-2.5-pro (Google) via direct providers.call_model. Verdict: VERIFIED_WITH_NOTES; all four claim-checks held true and the contract was judged sound. Dispositioned in-flight: IMPORTANT 'incomplete centralization' = ACKNOWLEDGED/OUT-OF-SCOPE (set's explicit non-goal; named CLAUDE.md sections already point to engine-agnostic canonical docs; follow-on candidate recorded); NICE-TO-HAVE consumer-table header drift = accepted into S2 residual scope. Raw output saved to s1-verification.md.

Authored disposition.json (status completed, verification_method api, verification_verdict VERIFIED_WITH_NOTES, next_orchestrator -> claude/anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 low for the S2 validation pass) and s1-close-reason.md; committed and pushed the Session 1 audit bundle; ran close_session on the API path to pass the five gates and flip Session 1 to complete.

Registered Session 2 start via ai_router.start_session with orchestrator metadata (claude / anthropic / claude-opus-4-8, effort high). Verified the on-disk orchestrator block matches the actual orchestrator.

Ran the three validation steps. (1) Confirmed the canonical docs/repository-reference.md -> 'Documentation authority and release status' section is present and well-formed (principle + consumer table + release-status table + recent walk) and cross-checked the release-status table against package metadata (pyproject 0.15.0, __init__ 0.15.0, package.json 0.27.0 all match). (2) Markdown render check clean: tables well-formed, heading nesting correct, engine-file Shared repo facts pointers reference the canonical section by prose title; a repo-wide grep for the #documentation-authority-and-release-status anchor returned zero live link uses so there is no broken-anchor risk. (3) Straggler grep sweep over LIVE docs (excluding historical session-sets, consumer-paste cross-repo notices, proposals, vscode-test fixtures, CHANGELOGs, this spec): found TWO live stragglers S1's narrower grep missed -- Finding A (HARD) docs/repository-reference.md:475 CLAUDE.md file-map row says shared facts 'live in this doc' contradicting the migration and inconsistent with sibling AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md rows; Finding B (SOFT) CONTRIBUTING.md:9 cites CLAUDE.md for 'the consumer-repo map'. S2 is validation-only and edits no document: both recorded with prescribed fixes and handed to Session 3. Authored s2-validation.md.

Ran an independent cross-provider verification focused on straggler-completeness (the crux of a validation session). Verifier: gemini-2.5-pro (Google) via direct providers.call_model. Raw verdict ISSUES_FOUND with one critical claiming the validation missed the consumer-table straggler (the ## Consumer repos table duplicated in all three engine files). Dispositioned in-flight as a context-gap FALSE POSITIVE against the locked S1 contract: s1-audit-record.md 3.3 explicitly permits a duplicated consumer table ('A short consumer table may be duplicated in the engine files for convenience'), the table's canonical copy verifiably exists in repository-reference.md so it is NOT sole-sourced, and the verifier was not fed s1-audit-record.md. Rather than dismiss it, re-cast the underlying 4-copies-of-the-table tension as an explicit keep-vs-remove DECISION for Session 3 (s2-validation.md 5 item 3, 6). Both claim checks that matter held true (canonical section well-formed; render clean); both S2 stragglers independently confirmed real; verifier endorsed S2's deferral discipline as 'sound'. Net S2 verdict VERIFIED_WITH_NOTES. Raw output saved to s2-verification.md.

Authored disposition.json (status completed, verification_method api, verification_verdict VERIFIED_WITH_NOTES, next_orchestrator -> claude/anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 for the Session 3 complete-centralization pass) and s2-close-reason.md; committed and pushed the Session 2 validation bundle; ran close_session --manual-verify (API path, verification ran out-of-band) to pass the gates and flip Session 2 to complete. Set stays in-progress for Session 3.

Registered Session 3 start via ai_router.start_session with orchestrator metadata (claude / anthropic / claude-opus-4-8, effort high). Verified the on-disk orchestrator block matches the actual orchestrator.

Enumerated every shared operational fact CLAUDE.md carried inline (richer than AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md) and located its engine-agnostic canonical home: orchestrator-block contract -> session-state-schema.md (s Per-session orchestrator block, s Writer Contract) + ai-led-session-workflow.md (D3 writer_discipline, P4 no-Explorer, orchestrator-writer.log) -- all verified present; Building & testing + Orchestrator e2e harness Layer 1/2/3 + CI -> CONTRIBUTING.md (s Test layers richer with the Set-045 rebuild trap, s CI, s Building); session-state schema summary -> session-state-schema.md (already named authoritative). Found the router-config-editor walkthrough was the ONLY genuinely sole-sourced fact with no engine-agnostic home (repository-reference.md's extension file map had no src/configEditor/ row).

Routed the punch-list item-3 consumer-table keep-vs-remove decision through decision-time cross-provider consensus (gemini-2.5-pro, google, independent of this claude/anthropic orchestrator, via direct providers.call_model; run_s3_consensus.py). Result: Option B (pointer-only), HIGH confidence -- drop the ## Consumer repos table from all three engine files in favor of the existing ## Shared repo facts pointer. Converges with the operator 'complete centralization, period' directive and the S2 verifier; the table had already drifted, demonstrating the duplication-for-convenience model fails for this fact. Decision LOCKED to B; makes punch-list item 4 (header drift) moot. Full trail: s3-consensus.md.

Executed the punch-list. Relocated the router-config-editor walkthrough into a new src/configEditor/ row in repository-reference.md's extension file map (command + 3 YAML files + key source files). Fixed Finding A (repository-reference.md:475 CLAUDE.md file-map row now says shared facts live in docs/repository-reference.md, aligned with sibling rows) and Finding B (CONTRIBUTING.md:9 cites CLAUDE.md only for role+portability and repository-reference.md for the canonical consumer map / release status). Rewrote CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md to a byte-identical shared body (## Quick start -> ## Decision-time consensus) differing only in the H1+audience header and a single final ## Engine-specific bootstrap section; dropped the consumer table (decision B); thinned CLAUDE.md's inline shared content to pointers; added the missing When-curator-work / Delegation-Discipline / Session-state-schema / Running-the-router sections to all three; upgraded the Shared repo facts pointers to clickable anchor links (item 6). CLAUDE.md 231->160 lines.

Validation (punch-list item 7). Structural diff: extracted ## Quick start .. ## Engine-specific bootstrap slice from each engine file -> sha256 identical (37242fe0...), 144 lines each, diff reports IDENTICAL across all three. No leftover ## Consumer repos / ## Orchestrator-block / Extension versioning / ai_router copy in any engine file. Straggler re-grep over live docs (excluding session-sets/proposals/cross-repo-notices/CHANGELOGs/this-set): zero live engine-file-as-canonical references; every remaining hit is a mechanism description, the correct post-migration principle, or consumer/new-project bootstrap. The lone implementation-summary-023-027.md CLAUDE.md mention is a historical Documentation-Updates summary (non-goal protects historical artifacts; S2-dispositioned out-of-scope) and was deliberately left untouched. Anchor #documentation-authority-and-release-status target exists (line 42) and all three files link it; code fences balanced; table rows well-formed. Authored s3-validation.md.

Ran end-of-session cross-provider verification. Verifier: gemini-2.5-pro (google) via direct providers.call_model (run_s3_verification.py), fed the actual post-rewrite engine files + full CONTRIBUTING.md + repository-reference.md AND the locked S1 contract s1-audit-record.md (supplied this time to close the S2 context gap that produced its false-positive critical) + the S3 consensus/validation records. Verdict: VERIFIED. All five claim checks held (symmetry; no leftover sole-sourced sections; Finding A fixed; Finding B fixed; relocation landed); verifier independently returned sole_sourced_facts [], lost_facts [], new_stragglers [] all empty, and endorsed the consumer-table removal as 'a sound tightening of the original contract.' No critical/important/nice-to-have findings; nothing to disposition. First run crashed on cp1252 stdout (the known ASCII-only-terminal gotcha) AFTER the paid call returned; fixed the script to write raw output to a UTF-8 file (s3-verification-raw.md) and re-ran. Records: s3-verification.md (formatted) + s3-verification-raw.md (raw JSON).

Authored disposition.json (status completed, verification_method api, verification_verdict VERIFIED, next_orchestrator reason.code 'other' = final session / set complete) and s3-close-reason.md; committed and pushed the Session 3 complete-centralization bundle; ran close_session --manual-verify (API path, verification ran out-of-band) to pass the gates, flip Session 3 to complete, and flip the SET to complete (final session). No code, no release.
